title: Profile nested hierarchical mtasks with verilator_gantt
body:
This PR introduces support for profiling nested mtasks in hierarchical verilation mode. Now mtasks from different hierarchical modules may use the same `taskId`s and are distinguished by their origin hierarchical module. Moreover, we may track estimation accuracy of top module mtasks that launch hierarchical ones. 

Excerpt from the new gantt report (`t_hier_block_perf`):

```
MTask statistics:                                                                                                  
  Longest mtask id = 12 from module 't'                                                                            
  Longest mtask time = 11.63% of time elapsed in parallelized code                                                 
  min log(p2e) = -1.529  from module 'Test' mtask 16 (predict 239, elapsed 1103)                                   
  max log(p2e) = 1.611  from module 't' mtask 31 (predict 14119, elapsed 2819)                                     
  mean = -0.969                                                                                                    
  stddev = 0.629                                                                                                   
  e ^ stddev = 1.875                                                                                               
```
Excerpt from emitted VCD visualised by GTKWave, note 0x0000000D and 0x0000000C outer tasks that started in `func nba` phase and launch smaller hierarchical tasks from the `Test` module.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fc967f8-c3b4-45e3-8b28-5618c0e5ce98)
